title: Level 437
wikitext:
{{New Theme}} {{Background|file=ChinaTown Hallway..jpg}}{{Content Warning|bad_content=Graphic imagery|page={{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 437: "Chinatown Mall"}} {{class 4|exit=Very Difficult to Exit|safety=Low Environmental Hazards|entity=Moderate Entity Count}} {{Color|background color=#d16a15|text color=white|border color=Black|border type=dotted|text= {{Quote|The feeling of being alone isn't something that was new to me after all my travels. But seeing the tinted windows and the signs in a familiar language I didn't understand made me feel something different. It was only until the lights went out and I heard her singing that I realized I didn't feel alone, but that I was the last person to be dragged away to a horrible place. Don't stop running until you find a store with its lights on; I don't think she can come in.|A notepad discovered in ''Level 437''.}}}} {{Underline}} '''Level 437''' is the 438th level of the backrooms. {{Blurimage|file=ChinaTown Hallway..jpg|caption=A photo of the Chinatown Mall hallways, taken by M.E.G. explorer.|align=center|width=400px}} ==General Description== __NOTOC__ Level 437 is a rather enigmatic level wanderers can find themselves in. When first entering the level, all wanderers will be within the middle of a Chinatown mall. The middle of the Chinatown mall is rather simple. With straightforward hallways that have signs and decorations in Mandarin or Mandarin and English. The walls may have black windows supposedly containing closed stores on the other side. It's possible to enter these closed stores either through the glass or doors, but one will quickly notice that doing so is unusually difficult. It's also recommended to not do this, as closed stores typically have nothing of value and might have nesting entities within them. Additionally, the level appears to be under a modified "isolation" effect, similar to the lobby. When within these Chinatown malls, it's impossible for a wanderer to come into contact with another. However, if one travels for long enough, they will come across open stores, otherwise known as 'subspaces.' In these stores/subspaces, wanderers are able to meet with other wanderers from different Chinatown malls from an impossibly long distance. One can enter someone else's subspace if that store has the same branding, which you can infer from the decorations within and outside the store. These subspaces can have a variety of items, from food and drinks on tables and counters to utility items, [[Object 1|almond water]], and even weapons in staff areas. In rare instances these subspaces will have a [[Entity 9|faceling]] "staff" or "host" who are happy to offer products of whatever store you're in for free. It's extremely recommended to not anger these facelings in any capacity, such as attacking, insulting, or entering restricted areas. As for whatever reason, these beings are able to 'will' you out of the subspace and deny you access to it again from outside. It's important to mention that these Chinatown malls act mostly Euclidean in nature, which means hallways won't shift, and subspaces are far and few between. So being denied access into a subspace is highly advised against, as it may leave you at the mercy of entities and phenomenons that we will discuss later. On the bright side, while these facelings try to maintain an air of professionalism, it is possible to get in their good graces. Wanderers have reported that doing this has led to these facelings giving them directions to the entrance of the mall or other useful advice. After enough exploration, since the Chinatown mall doesn't shift, a wanderer will eventually reach the entrance. {{Blurimage|file=Entrance Stairway..jpg|caption=The highly enigmatic stairway found in the main entrance.|align=center|width=400px}} The three most noteworthy details of the entrance are the nearby stores, the second-floor stairway, and the faceling greeter. The nearby stores within the entrance are always closed/run down; however, they never contain nesting entities. The only entities ever reported to be seen within the mall entrance being facelings. The second-floor stairway within the main entrance is considerably stranger. When climbing the stairway, attempting to cross the top threshold will be like passing through an illusion; after fully passing through, it will bring one to the front of the entrance. All attempts to access the second floor besides the stairway have been tried and failed. Interaction/communication with the greeter faceling is mostly minimal, with the most common response being, "Welcome to the mall; we hope you enjoy your visit." Upon further on-the-field experimentation, it was revealed that the greeter faceling is more dangerous than the ones found in subspaces. Attacking or putting the greeter faceling in danger will result in it calling for "security." To which multiple facelings will materialize from the top of the main entrance stairs, wielding lethal weaponry such as firearms, tasers, and machetes. These security guards will attack any aggressors still inside the mall, killing them and then chopping them into pieces with any on-hand blades. Afterwards they will carry as many pieces as they can and return to the top of the stairs, where they will dematerialize. ==The Outside== The second area of level 437, just beyond the doors of the Chinatown mall main entrance, is The Outside. The immediate surroundings when exiting the mall for every wanderer is the outside of the mall. One might notice that the outside of the mall is visibly smaller than what was traveled within. There are also a variety of stores on the outside, with all of them being closed and run down. Though the outside closed stores aren't as hard to enter as the ones inside the mall. There is also a second floor to the outside, which can easily be accessed. After investigation, there seems to be no relation between the outside second floor and the second floor within the mall, and thus it is still inaccessible. {{Blurimage|file=Outside Second Floor..jpg|caption=The closed stores commonly seen on the outside of Chinatown malls.|align=center|width=400px}} The point beyond the property of the Chinatown mall is the infinite parking lot. A parking lot that visibly stretches beyond a person's vision. Streetlights, signs, palm trees, curbs, indicators, and vehicles can be found within this second area. The structure of the parking lot becomes progressively more chaotic and illogical the farther a person travels from a Chinatown mall compared to parking lots found on [[The Frontrooms|Earth]]. Vehicles that are found are commonly sedans and SUVs, or any other vehicle that doesn't exceed the size of a semi-truck. The functionality of these vehicles varies greatly, with some missing entire parts or only needing minor repairs. Food and supplies can occasionally be found inside these vehicles, with documentation even being found in areas such as glove boxes. However, these documentations are typically written in gibberish, and any identified individuals in these documents seem to have never existed. The second area is also an exception to the modified "isolation" effect, where any amount of wanderers can meet each other within this area in Euclid fashion. As that implies, there are multiple Chinatown malls within the infinite parking lot that can be located and entered, with supposedly their own unique internal structure. Excluding the main entrance, which every mall seems to have in the exact same form. It's unknown whether there is a limit to how many malls exist in Level 437 or if they materialize when a wanderer first enters. The Outside additionally has a functioning day and night cycle, though there are no celestial bodies ever seen; it'll be day for twenty hours and night for twelve. Whenever it becomes dark out, night-dwelling entities such as [[Entity 8|Hounds]], [[Entity 3|Smilers]], and [[Entity 4|Deathmoths]] can be spotted all over The Outside. Hiding out in malls', outside stores, or vehicles is usually an effective method to avoid these entities. The final exception to the "isolation" effect is egress into the malls. Experimentation revealed that when wanderers enter a mall within a 1-5 second time frame, they are able to exist within it at the same time. However, attempting to enter the same mall after witnessing this phenomenon seemingly transports that person to a different mall. Don't do this. ==Entities== ===[[Entity 10|'''Skin-Stealers''']]=== Potentially one of the first entities that can be encountered in this level is the skin-stealer. The entity, while normal in its biology, behaves significantly differently compared to skin-stealers found on other levels. Reports have never included a skin-stealer without human skin already on them. These skin-stealers are also seen wearing clothing that a customer would wear. When being seen by them, they will hurriedly pass by a wanderer and walk out of view down a hallway. Sometimes even attempting to make what is believed to be a passing comment, though they cannot actually speak. These entities are still to be treated with extreme caution, as the slightest touch will provoke them to enter their rage mode and violently tear apart offenders. When trying to follow these entities to see where they're going, being spotted multiple times will result in the same reaction. It's as if skin-stealers aren't interested in consuming skin at all. Due to the unpredictability of these entities, it's suggested to steer clear of them. They've only been seen within area one of the level, so avoiding them is fairly easy. ===[[Entity 5|'''Clumps''']]=== The next entity that acts uniquely within this level is the clump. These entities are mostly seen in closed stores and vehicles waiting to ambush unsuspecting wanderers. The best way to avoid these entities is to notice abnormalities in your surroundings. Such as doors left ajar, broken store windows, and vehicles with their windows rolled down. It's important to remember that within a Chinatown mall, closed stores are hard to enter, so a store that can be easily entered is likely a trap. Cars are also commonly broken down, so if one is functional enough to have its window rolled down, a clump may be attempting to make it inviting for an ambush. Stores on the outside of malls that are already easy to enter may also have clumps, so it's important to stay vigilant around these stores since it's impossible to discern if they're trapped. Clumps supposedly have the ability, or knowledge, to turn on the lights in closed stores. This can be especially dangerous when a wanderer needs a subspace to hide from a phenomenon, possibly landing directly in a trap instead of a haven. If a subspace is fake, you will be able to see inside of it and its dilapidated nature, so stay perceptive for these tricks. ==='''Night-Dwellers'''=== The entities that can be found outside during the night are the Hounds, Smilers, and Deathmoths. These entities act as they normally would, with the biggest risk being the sheer amount of them during the night. The Deathmoths are the only other entity that can be found in the first area inside closed stores. With male and female Deathmoths nesting and secreting [[[Object 6|moth jelly]]. ===[[Entity 9|'''Facelings''']]=== While they’re the most unique entity to the level, the facelings' enigmatic nature has left us with little information. Since this is the case, we’ve broken down what we have gathered into three types of facelings. ===='''Staff Facelings'''==== These neutral facelings are able to manipulate the rules of reality-bending areas in level 437. Either being able to deny access to subspaces for certain wanderers or seemingly summon other facelings from the illusory stairs in the main entrance. Though they are more commonly seen performing whatever job they've been stationed to to the letter. These facelings are generally harmless and can even be helpful, leaving no reason for hostility towards them. ===='''Security Guard Facelings'''==== These facelings are only ever seen when certain staff are in the most dire of circumstances. They can have a variety of lethal to non-lethal weapons, having protection equivalent to a security guard. With at least one in the group having a bladed weapon for chopping apart aggressors. These security guard facelings are able to cooperate and operate with the same effectiveness as marines. Able to advance towards a threat with self-preservation appearing to be a second priority, without compromising the goal at hand. It's advised to avoid provoking these facelings appearance. ===='''Butcher Facelings'''==== The butcher facelings are entities only seen during the "festival hour" phenomenon. The facelings will travel in large groups of 10-15 through the hallways of Chinatown malls. Reports indicate that they wear garments suggesting they have the job of a butcher, exterminator, farmer, or any other occupation involving animal slaughter. They always hold bloodied and dull blades no bigger than a bowie machete and have about the same combat skill as facelings found on other levels, unlike the security guard facelings. These facelings are the most aggressive out of the three types. They'll surround wanderers and forcefully bring them to the ground. Where they then viciously cut and chop their victims into pieces. ==='''The Mother'''=== The Mother is a level-specific entity, only encounterable on Level 437 during the ‘blackout’ phenomenon. There have been very few reported encounters with this entity due to the fact that it is believed to be nearly impossible to survive her when seeing the entity. The Mother has the appearance of the Japanese yokai rokurokubi. Her neck being able to extend to unbelievable distances, her face is similar to a partial faceling, with her mouth, nose, and hair being the only body parts on her face. She appears to have rows of razor-sharp teeth but prefers to attack by crushing wanderers with her neck, similar to a boa constrictor. Her neck and face are unusually difficult to harm, with bullets and blades unable to make puncture wounds. However, additional encounters with her have revealed her neck to be susceptible to blunt force. While a majority of people are mercilessly killed by the entity, anyone with East Asian, South Asian, Pacific Islander, Asian American, or other Asian descent is spared. The Mother will take on a human form that’s regarded as “very beautiful” before offering the spared wanderer help with whatever they need (being that it's quick) and even offering to take these wanderers in. ==Phenomena== ===“Festival Hour”=== On rare occasions when traveling in the first area, this phenomenon will trigger. You’ll know that this phenomenon is about to begin when the lights change color into an orange, festive tint. A happy-go-lucky shopping tune will play from unseen speakers, and the Chinatown hallways will have a joyful but uncanny atmosphere. Don’t let that atmosphere trick you, as wanderers have about a minute to either find a subspace or hide in a closed store. When that time is up, a flow of hostile entities will start to fill the hallways. Firstly, most clumps will abandon their hiding spots and attempt to ram or tackle individuals off their feet. Then skin-stealers, now holding briefcases, will violently bludgeon wanderers caught outside. While it seems these entities aren’t afraid to kill wanderers, when the final entity, the butcher facelings, arrive. The skin-stealers move out of their way so they can finish off the wanderers. Pushing them to the floor if they aren’t already from the skin stealers assault, before then chopping them to pieces, a process that usually takes up the rest of the phenomenon’s duration. Which can last from a half an hour to an hour. When the entities are finished, they’ll pick up the pieces of the dead wanderer(s) and walk out of sight. Returning the hallways to their common empty atmosphere. While the exact chance for this phenomenon to occur hasn’t been figured out, it’s believed that consistent travel through the Chinatown hallways for 3 days guarantees this event's occurrence. ===“Blackout”=== For the few times this event was recorded, it was only in the first area of the level. This event will start with the lights in the hallways going out and the surrounding environment becoming pitch black. Wanderers have a limited time to find a subspace; hiding in closed stores will NOT work as a solution in this instance. While it’s unknown what the time limit is, some have said they felt like they were looking for a subspace for fifteen minutes. A person will begin to feel their time limit being reached when the sound of a woman singing a rhyme or other tune is heard from the hallway behind them. You MUST find a subspace when this singing is heard; if you don’t, then you will be seen by The Mother. By that point you are dead. It’s believed that the entity has abilities similar to the staff facelings. Because when seen, wanderers who try to flee further will appear to be in a hexagonal “loop.” Where it is physically impossible to escape the entity. When locating a subspace, a wanderer will be able to wait out the phenomenon and survive, a method that also works with “festival hour.” It’s currently unknown what the likelihood of this phenomenon triggering is. ===“Transformation”=== This phenomenon can technically trigger at any point within the level, but it is most commonly seen in subspaces and the infinite parking lot. When exploring vehicles or subspaces for food and loot, there is a possibility to come across drinks and liquids other than almond water. It is worth noting that these drinks are more common than almond water within this level, likely for the purpose of enticing wanderers to trigger this phenomenon. When any liquid other than almond water is consumed, a painful process will begin that lasts from one to two hours. By the end, the afflicted person will fully transform into a faceling and will attempt to flee from their group (if they're with one) until they are completely out of sight. A more disturbing side effect of this phenomenon is that if a group tries to prevent the faceling from running away, it will thrash and shake aggressively, as if in distress. If held back for long enough, the entity will send out telepathic links that can severely harm the psyche of those within close proximity. The sound of these links often being attributed to a smoke alarm screaming its head off if it were a person. ===“Shopping Fever”=== This phenomenon is the most subtle of the four seen within this level. After several experimentations, it’s believed that looting 2-3 subspaces will cause a wanderer to contract this “fever.” Less severe symptoms take about five days to form. These symptoms may include thoughts of finding and exploring subspaces, the urge to talk with host facelings, cravings for unique foods found in subspaces, and rising feelings of overconfidence. Observation of individuals with this fever has led to the belief that this phenomenon is more of an addiction. Where afflicted persons will try to come up with reasonings or excuses for looting subspaces. Attempts have been made to have afflicted wanderers loot vehicles found in the infinite parking lot as a safer measure of satisfying the addiction. Admittedly, the supplies found in subspaces are very generous, comparable to even certain habitual levels. For this reason it's believed that while the method of looting vehicles can hold back severe symptoms for a time, it is not a permanent solution. If this addiction persists without the necessary stimulation of looting subspaces, then more severe symptoms will begin to appear. Severe symptoms of this fever include itchiness, yellowing of the eyes, abdominal pain, mood swings, weight loss, and minor spasms alongside the minor symptoms. It's worth noting that even when severe symptoms appear, looting subspaces will cause all of them to disappear. But if the last and worst symptom surfaces, it's believed that they won't disappear even after looting subspaces. The final symptom is a sudden development of "feral human," followed by the wanderer abandoning their group and fleeing into the nearest Chinatown mall, almost never to be seen again. While all of this is incredibly worrisome, the bright side is that removing an afflicted person from Level 437 appears to make the fever disappear for good. There have been no reports on whether this method cures those in the final stage of "shopping fever," though. And this method of curing infected wanderers doesn't make them immune to "shopping fever." However, a self-awareness may be gained afterwards, hindering the effects. ==Colonies== ===Subspace Seekers=== *A group of about 56 ‘official members’. *Friendly towards wanderers and certain large scale factions. Willing to trade. *Focused on searching for subspaces. Will leave the symbol of their faction on walls, which leads to nearby subspaces. *Directly oppose the “Lot Lords” and are disliked by The Mother’s Children. *Have assisted M.E.G. with certain operations in level 437, but are generally isolationists. *Have said to be on a “mission” that involves the subspaces. They may no longer exist after their mission is finished. ===The Lot Lords=== *Patrols the infinite parking lot for lonely wanderers, forcing whoever they find into slavery. *A large scale population believed to be over 250. Including slaves. *A majority of the population is infected with “shopping fever.” *Force captured wanderer’s to search malls for subspaces, while actual members scavenge for vehicles. *Hostile towards every Level 437 unique faction, the M.E.G., and uncooperative wanderer’s. *Discovered that severe symptoms of shopping fever abate if slaves return to them loot found in subspaces. This has limited effectiveness with how large the faction is. ===The Mother’s Children=== *A small group of around 35 members and growing. *All members are individuals of asian descent taken in by The Mother entity. *Are somehow settled beyond the illusory stairs in the main entrance, living in a store on the second floor inside a Chinatown mall. *Members are sometimes seen doing ‘errands’ for The Mother or looking for those of Asian descent to recruit. *Became hostile to the Lot Lords after they killed a member, but became hostile with M.E.G. after incident 43. *It’s believed that the members of the faction have completely abandoned escaping the backrooms. ===M.E.G. Outpost 43=== *Friendly towards wanderers but don’t take in new members. *Inform visiting wanderers of methods on how to escape level 437. *Secured the entire outside perimeter/roof of a Chinatown mall, acting as the outpost. *Returned functionality to 20 patrol vehicles. *Responsible for most of the information gathered and published on this page. *Killed The Mother entity of the mall they’ve secured.
{{POI|name=Alan Huáng|status=Alive|location=Level 427|affliations=[[M.E.G.|M.E.G.]]|seen=Base Phi}}
Huáng zǐ ruì, English name: Alan Huáng, is a M.E.G. operative most well known for his remarkable actions on level 437. He was stationed in outpost 43. Being assigned to expeditions or participating in minor experiments. Until one day when, with a small group of three, they had been caught in a "blackout" event. Alan and his group were unable to locate a subspace, and his two fellow operatives were killed in front of him. In an unbelievable act of composure, when The Mother offered to take Alan in, he had accepted. Alan infiltrated the faction "The Mother's Children," but was quickly disturbed by the cult-like following the members had developed around the entity. There was hardly any structure to the faction. Every member that Alan did interact with saw the entity as a genuine motherly figure and revered it with the same respect of an actual mother. From Alan's interactions and observations of the entity, she really does act the part. The Mother behaves very supportively to her "children," actively looks to take care of members, and is open to most questions. Her supportiveness was truly put to the test, however, when Alan had asked to leave the faction and return to his old one. Which she had agreed to even though it "saddened" her. Alan's return to outpost 43 and his subsequent debriefing were shocking. With all the information he gathered being put on level 437’s page immediately. After this, Alan was quickly promoted and relocated to [[level 427|level 427]] in base phi. This wasn’t the end of his encounters with “The Mother’s Children,” though. Sometime after his relocation, a member of TMC was spotted attempting to kill Alan Huáng in his sleep on base phi. When interrogated on why they had tried this, they only responded with, “No matter how far Huáng zǐ ruì runs, he will see mother again. Whether it’s today or tomorrow. She has a lot to discuss with him.” Neutralization was quickly exercised after this. Beyond that, no incidents with TMC have occurred since, and Alan can still be found on base phi. Here is some additional information that Alan uncovered that didn’t fit in the excerpt: *The store that TMC is settled in is seemingly infinite. *Members of TMC are able to locate each other in the store, even though it’s infinite. Alan didn’t learn how to do this. *The Mother makes routine trips for almond water, since her “children” can’t drink the water in level 437. *The Mother will occasionally bring back cooked backshroom dishes, though she’s never seen cooking. *Many of the members seem to have significantly lost their personalities. *Members seem to only be interested in their own devices, socially interacting more with the entity. *When The Mother is around the TMC she is always in her human form. *There are supposedly other stores on the second floor, but The Mother is the only one who’s allowed to visit them. *The Mother revealed that her form is “merely an extension” and that every Chinatown mall has only one of this extension. *When asked as to why The Mother spares those of Asian descent but not others, she said, “Oh honey, I'm not hurting the people that I don’t "spare.” I’m only helping them with their inability to see the beauty.” *The Mother called the Subspace Seekers "interesting." The Lot Lord's "poor little saps I wish I could help." And the M.E.G. " very pushy busybodies."
{{Collapse|num=1|initial=
'''Open Incident 43?'''
|final= '''Close Incident 43''' |body=
'''''Incident 43'''''
Incident 43 is a series of events that transpired after the return of the M.E.G. explorer Alan Huáng. The culmination of these events is most commonly referred to as '''the''' incident. Once Alan Huáng and his story had reached the upper management of Outpost 43, the leadership of the outpost reached out to centralized M.E.G. leadership. Requesting permission to interview a member of "The Mother's Children" alongside recording equipment. The request would be granted. After extensive efforts to communicate with "The Mother's Children" if they would agree to an interview, they had accepted. A subspace would become the agreed-upon location, and a faceling host would act as a mediator. A team of six would be sent to the agreed spot with a rather uneventful passage. The climax of this incident has been saved as a recording. There are moments that are highly disturbing; listen at your own risk. ... ... ... <**LOADING**> ... ... <*CLICK*> : Command, this is team leader. Can you hear me? I think we have everyone's channel open. : We hear you loud and clear. Houston, have you reached th- : HI command! : Hi Caleb. While your channels are open, can everyone try to speak one at a time? : Yes, sorry, command. : Can you repeat that command? : Have you reached the destination? : Without a hitch, and we've got our guest of honor here too. : Can one of you give the guest a microphone so we can record their responses? : Yeah, I've got it. : It's nothing to worry about; it's only there to remember what's happened here, so our people back where we came from can hear it for themselves. : What was that, Boyd? Who are you speaking to? : To the host we have here. He was asking about what the equipment does, just cooling his nerves. : Alright, I think I've got our guest all set up. : Have them speak into the microphone to see if they're coming through. : Can you try speaking into the fluffy part there? : Chén dá speaking. : It looks like everything's working as it should. Before we continue, remember to speak one at a time. : I'll keep the boys in line for you command. : You wouldn't be team leader if we doubted that. Now proceed. : I have a few questions about your group and yourself, if that's okay with you? : Yes, that's fine. : You and your group are what many others would call a ‘faction.’ If you were to consider your friends as a faction, what would be its mission statement? : … : We will help as many lost souls that come through this place. : If that’s your cause, then why does the entity harm people? In what way is that helping? : You already know the answer to that; next question. : What do you mean we already know the answer? : Exactly what I just said. I don’t intend to repeat myself, so quit prying. : O-okay… Many factions of the backrooms are established in multiple levels, whereas yours seems to still be at the stage of conception. Do you plan to expand to other levels in the future? : No. We have as much control as we need already. : The level we’re in is very large, though. Do you really not need to expand to accomplish your goal further? : No, next question. : ... Many of the people in the backrooms are trying to find their way back home. What is it that the entity offers that changes your mind? : I’m not trying to find a way home because this ‘’is’’ my home. : Is that because of the entity? What does she do th- : Didn’t you hear me? I said I won’t repeat myself. : If you answered our questions more clearly, you wouldn’t have to. : Are you trying to make this difficult for us? : That’s enough interrupting the interview, Boyd. : That’s right. I don’t owe anything to you to begin. I’ll answer your questions as I want. : We all came here risking our lives to perform this interview. So be professional about this, and then we can go home. : (Chuckles) The only one whose life is in danger is yours. : What the hell is that supposed to mean? : What do you think I mean? : We’re completely wasting our time with this person. It was a mistake to do this interview. : Command, can we return to base at this point? We’re not getting anything out of Chén. : Are we completely forgetting what I just said? : Everything that he’s said only gives us more questions than answers, Houston. It doesn’t even seem like he wants to be here, and I don’t feel very comfortable with this person either. : Based on the responses so far. We’ve deemed that you should leave the situation. : Roger that command. : We understand that you might not be comfortable disclosing certain information. But if your faction agreed to this, I was under the impression you were willing to alleviate some of our questions. : Why did you agree to this interview? : ... : Maybe I just wanted to see you be frustrated. To test how far I could make this insufferable for you so I could see your pathetic facade crumble apar- … : You’re right. I shouldn’t keep mother waiting by losing control of myself. : Were you just… : I came to do this interview for only one purpose: to deliver a message. : We welcome you to our home, relieve you of your inabilities, and you throw away that hospitality for your “duties.” Spill our secrets to the world and document us like we’re animals. We don’t like the way that you operate or the delusional entitlement you believe you have over others. : We, The Mother’s Children, declare war on you. : And when you walk out that door… you’ll be caught in a blackout. … : What… just happened? : Should we leave? He said we’d be in a blackout if we left. : Command? : You should return to base; we don’t believe Chén can predict phenomenons, even if he’s with an entity. : We still have this subspace even if there was one. : Let’s go then; finish packing up. : Keep your channels open for this trip back. ... : Whatever you say. The team leaves the subspace a moment later. For about 10 seconds the hallway is normal until a "blackout" phenomenon triggers. : Everyone head back to the subspace; we'll wait this out. : The door won't open! : How is that possible? We didn't do anything to anger the faceling. : Get out of the way! : That obviously wasn't going to work. What the hell do we do? : Can you hear that singing? Is the entity already near us!? : Jesus fucking christ, I can't believe we're going to die this way. : Everyone calm down! Command, what do we do? : Run in the opposite direction of the singing. You may be able to find an exit to level fifty-nine. : You heard command, GO GO! : AUGH. : Houston, what happened to Marcus? : What? He should be behind us. : Did he not make the corner? : I'll get him! : JOSEPH, DON'T RUN OFF WITHOUT THE TEAM! : Where are they? They were just around this corner! : ''Oh, I found you.'' Oh my god. : Marcus, stay down! : Houston, where are Joseph and Marcus!? : We can't see them! But we can hear them shooting! It's like they're right next to us! : JOSEPH! MARCUS! : We need to leave! We can't help them! : We can't just leave them! They're right next to us! : Listen to your leader! Marcus and Joseph are a lost cause. : Keep moving! The team flees from the screams of their dying friends. For around four minutes the phenomenon persists until the lights come back. The team is silent as they begin to catch their breaths. Until a minute later, another phenomenon starts. : Festival hour!? : The whole level is out to get us! : I can't take this anymore; why did this have to happen to us!? : We have to keep moving; I won't let any more of you die! : LIGHTS! : (HUFF.) Wait! (cough.) That's not a real subspace! : Boyd, stop! : UGH! : HOUSTON! : Caleb! What are you doing? : Don't try to help them! We're all just going to die. We need to hide in a store! : Our friends need us! : Please don't go! If clumps are showing up, then skin-stealers will too; I don't want to watch you die! ... : Let's hide there! <*CLICK*> ... ... This concludes the recording of the team's exact moments; however, the scene can still be described. Houston was able to fight off the clump but was brutally beaten by skin-stealers as they arrived. Whereas Boyd was dragged out of the fake store by butcher facelings. The two surviving operatives had to watch as Houston and Boyd were cut apart, screaming until they lost their necks to do so. The whole event lasted for an hour as skin-stealers stood around and watched. Eventually the pieces of Houston and Boyd were put into the pockets of the butcher facelings. As they left, the skin-stealers stuffed the remains into their briefcases, clasping them shut, and walking away. The remaining operatives returned to outpost 43 and were given a vacation of five weeks to a habitual level of their choice.
}} File:Outside picture of the entrance.jpg|The outside appearance of a mall main entrance. File:ChinaTown stairway .jpg|A M.E.G. explorer's outside descent. File:Second Floor infinite store.jpg|The infinite store that the TMC resides in. File:Subspace Seeker symbol..jpg|Recovered sign of the Subspace Seekers symbol.
==Entrances and Exits== ===Entrances=== *Hearing the sound of a young woman singing in [[Level 11|Level 11]] and following the voice into a tight and dark alleyway will bring one to this level. *Within the first 20 minutes of the flood in [[level 11.11|level 11.11]], one must swim to the bottom of a street under the surface of the water. Afterwards you must lift a manhole cover and swim until you cannot see light anymore. After a minute, or until you pass out, you will drop into the middle of a Chinatown mall. *Finding a lonesome door in a large dark spot on [[level 26|level 26]] with the numbers 1314 on the top will bring one to this level. *Finding VHS tapes of old chinese films found on earth in a shelved room on [[level 40|level 40]] and playing/watching the full thing in a theater room will bring one to this level. Some reports have indicated that level 40's "paranoia" effect worsens when watching a film, so this method of egress into the level is unrecommended. *In the scenario where a wanderer falls through the chains of [[level 192|level 192]], they may rarely end up here. ===Exits=== *If one is able to repair a car to functionality and drive its maximum miles per hour for twenty seconds without colliding into objects or spinning out. Then the scenery around them will change, and they will be transported to [[level 50|level 50]] with their vehicle. Don't try this, please. *Sometimes, very rarely, one can find a car in absolute perfect condition in the infinite parking lot. Entering this car and sitting in either seat for ten second will cause one to pass out for the slightest moment, regaining conciousness and awakening in a broken derilic car a small distance away from a [[level 711|7 Eleven]]. It's worth noting entering these perfect cars and leaving the level makes these cars disappear. *In highly rare instances when it seems like the "festival hour" phenomena is about to begin, instead, cylinder-like lanterns will float into view and fill the hallway. These lanterns may have small open flames, but even in close proximity, do not burn the skin. If one stays in this strange occurrence for long enough, the level will shift into [[level 909|level 909]] in the middle of a Shangyuan Festival. *There has been a single report of a painted green mountainous landscape found in a closed store that noclips you to the start of [[level 909|level 909]]. *During the "blackout" event, if a person explicitly chooses to ignore a subspace, their only chance of survival, and continue running for long enough without being seen. An out-of-place hallway may appear, which will lead to [[level 59|''The Sins of a Massive Mall.'']] *On random occasions, if one is passing through a mall alone, and only alone, they may find a large bunker-like door that can be twisted open. Entering will involve climbing a flight of stairs until you reach another door. Which will bring one to the top of a building in the middle of the hazardous zone in [[level 998|level 998]]. If you ignore this door at the mall but change your mind and turn around, it will be gone, suggesting it's a sort of "one time offer." *When traveling through the infinite parking lot, retrieving a picture of a young boy/girl from the glove box of a car and then no-clipping into it will bring one to [[level 675|level 675]]. *Very recently, the Subspace Seekers confirmed the extremely rare existence of "wormholes" in subspaces. They have only found one, which, from what they said. Sent their explorer to a [[level 87|'''''paradise''''']]. {{Collapse|num=2|initial= '''Open Author & Credits Info''' |final= '''Close Author & Credits Info''' |body= '''Author:''' [[User:Mr.PartyCrasher|Mr.PartyCrasher]]}}}} [[Category:Cluster I]] [[Category:Levels]] [[Category:Dangerous]]
